doc.help: Document viewer. Use up and down arrows to move through the list of sections. When a section has sub-topics, press right arrow or Enter to open it and move into the deeper list, and press left arrow or Backspace to go back to the previous list. Press Tab to move to the text area and read the selected section line by line, then Shift plus Tab to return to the list. Press Escape to close.
about.title: About Kinetix Mod Manager
about.windowTitle: About Kinetix Mod Manager - Press Escape to Close
about.body:
Kinetix Mod Manager
Version: {0}
Publisher: Audi Venture Games
Website: https://github.com/SeanTerry01/Kinetix-Mod-Manager

Kinetix Mod Manager is a fully keyboard-driven, screen-reader-accessible mod manager for Stardew Valley, Skyrim Special Edition, and Fallout 4. It is built for the blind and visually impaired gaming community, with spoken feedback, full keyboard control, and audio cues throughout.

With it you can install and manage your mods, check for and apply updates through Nexus Mods, discover new mods, save and switch between mod profiles, keep automatic backups, browse an accessible game wiki, and review the SMAPI log for Stardew Valley.

Kinetix Mod Manager works with NVDA, JAWS, and SAPI-based screen readers via Tolk.

Copyright (C) 2026 Audi Venture Games.
Provided as-is for personal and community use.

Press left arrow or Backspace to collapse back to the previous list. Press Control plus E to edit a mod's configuration when available. Press Escape to close.

suite.engineFixesManualSpeak: SSE Engine Fixes must be downloaded manually from Nexus. Opening browser.
suite.engineFixesManualBox:
SSE Engine Fixes has two files on this page:

1. Main file: download it, then press Control+I in the mod manager to install it.

2. "Engine Fixes - skse64 Preloader": download it and extract d3dx9_42.dll directly into your Skyrim game folder (the folder containing SkyrimSE.exe).

wiki.noCategories: No categories — browse this wiki in the view

helpers.pressEnterOpenPage: . Press Enter to open mod page.

help.installed: Installed Mods: Space to Toggle. Delete to remove mod. {0} to Search. {1} to change Category. {2} to batch toggle Category. {3} for Nexus. {4} for dependencies. {5} to Quick-Fix. {6} for Nexus ID. {7} to install zip. {8} to save as profile. {9} to read description. {10} to edit the mod's config file. {11} to edit the mod's manifest file. {12} to launch game.
help.updates: Updates: Enter for Nexus page. Delete to Ignore this update. {0} to Update All (Premium). {1} to read description. {2} to launch game.
help.backups: Backups: Enter to restore. Delete to remove zip. {0} to prune old backups. {1} to open backups folder.
help.discovery: Search for Mods: Enter opens the Nexus page, or loads more results when on the Load more row at the bottom of the list. {0} to read summary. Tab to search.
help.wiki: Stardew Wiki: Type in Search box and press Enter. Or select a Category. In Results: Enter to open page or sub-category. Backspace to go back up. Tab into the web view to read content with screen reader commands (H for headings, T for tables).
help.walkthroughs: {0} Walkthroughs: Select a guide from the list using Up/Down arrow keys. Press F6 or Tab to move to the Web View to read the guide with screen reader commands (H for headings, T for tables).
help.profiles: Profiles: Enter to apply profile. Delete to remove profile.
help.modPriority: Mod Priority: this list decides which mod's files win when two mods change the same file. The mod at the top of the list has the highest priority and wins. Use the Up and Down arrows to move through the list, and Control plus Up or Control plus Down to move the selected mod higher or lower in priority. Each mod reads how many files it overrides or is overridden in.
help.pluginOrder: Plugin Order: this is the load order of your plugin files, the dot e s p, dot e s m, and dot e s l files, written to plugins dot t x t. Plugins higher in the list load first. Masters and light masters always load before regular plugins, and the base game and its add-ons load automatically and are not listed here. Use the Up and Down arrows to move through the list, and Control plus Up or Control plus Down to move the selected plugin earlier or later. A plugin cannot move across the boundary between masters and regular plugins. Press {0} to auto-sort, which arranges every plugin to load after the masters it needs.
help.gameLog: Log: View the logs your game and its script-extender plugins write, such as f4se dot log, the per-mod logs, and your accessibility mod's log. Use the Log dropdown to choose which file to view, the Filter dropdown for Full Log or Errors and Warnings, and the Search box to find text. Arrow through the lines. {0} refreshes the log, even while the game is running. Control C copies the selected lines. {1} opens the raw file.
help.creations: Creations: Bethesda Creations are downloaded inside the game from its Creations menu, or queued from the Bethesda dot net website, never by a mod manager. This list shows the Creations already installed in your game's Data folder, whether each is active, and if it is a master or light master. Use the Up and Down arrows to move through the list, and press Space to activate or deactivate the selected Creation. To change their load order, use the Plugin Order tab.
help.smapiLog: SMAPI Log: Filter dropdown includes Errors Only, Errors and Warnings, Full Log, and Links Only. Search box available. Enter on a line with a link opens it, or lists the links to choose from when there are several; Enter on a search result jumps to it in full view. {0} to diagnose and fix the selected line. Control C to copy selected lines to the clipboard. {1} to refresh the log, even while the game is running. {2} to upload to SMAPI.io. {3} to open raw file.
loadorder.atTop: Already at the top, highest priority.
loadorder.atBottom: Already at the bottom, lowest priority.
loadorder.masterBoundary: Masters must load before regular plugins. Cannot move across that boundary.

loadorder.rebuildConfirm: Rebuild the mod deployment for the current game? This re-links every enabled mod's files into the game folder and rewrites the plugin list. It does not delete any of your mods.

loadorder.exportError: Could not export the load order: {0}
loadorder.importConfirm: Replace the current load order with the imported one? Your installed mods are not added or removed; only their priority and plugin order change. Entries for anything you do not have installed are ignored.
loadorder.imported: Imported load order: {0} mods and {1} plugins. {2} file conflicts resolved.

loadorder.importInvalid: That file does not contain a load order.
loadorder.importGameMismatch: That load order is for {0}, but {1} is currently loaded. Load the matching game first.
mo2.title: Import from Mod Organizer 2
mo2.notApplicable: Importing from Mod Organizer 2 only applies to Skyrim and Fallout 4.
mo2.needModsPath: Set the mods folder for this game in Settings before importing.
mo2.pickFolder: Select your Mod Organizer 2 folder (the one containing the mods and profiles folders).
mo2.notFound: That folder does not look like a Mod Organizer 2 setup. It must contain a mods folder and a profiles folder.
mo2.gameMismatch: That Mod Organizer 2 instance is set up for {0}, but {1} is currently loaded. Load the matching game first.
mo2.noProfiles: No Mod Organizer 2 profiles with a mod list were found.
mo2.noMods: That profile has no mods to import.
mo2.confirm: Import the Mod Organizer 2 profile "{0}"? This copies {1} mods, {2} of them enabled, into Kinetix Mod Manager and applies their order and plugin list. Your Mod Organizer 2 setup is not changed. Mods you already have in the manager are skipped.
mo2.importing: Importing from Mod Organizer 2. This may take a while for large mod lists.
mo2.done: Imported {0} mods from Mod Organizer 2: {1} enabled, {2} disabled. {3} active plugins. {4} skipped as already present, {5} not found.
ui.resultsPerLoad: Results to load at a time
ui.modPriorityDesc: Highest priority first. Control plus Up or Down moves the selected mod.
ui.pluginOrderDesc: Load order, first loaded at the top. Control plus Up or Down moves the selected plugin.
ui.creationsDesc: Bethesda Creations installed in the game. Space activates or deactivates the selected Creation. Reorder them on the Plugin Order tab.
sound.connect: Played when successfully connected to Nexus Mods.
sound.disconnect: Played when you are disconnected, need to enter an API key, or when the program closes.
sound.enable: Played when one or more mods are enabled.
sound.disable: Played when one or more mods are disabled or deleted.
sound.error: Played when an error occurs, such as a failed download.
sound.loading_indicator: A pulsing sound that plays while the manager is checking for updates in the background.
sound.load_complete: Played when the manager has finished checking all mods for updates.
menu.autoMatch: Auto-match Nexus IDs for All Mods

fomod.none: None - install nothing from this group
